title: Run Claude
subtitle: Spin up a Claude Code session right inside the dashboard. Live streaming output, multi-turn conversation, and the same hooks-driven analytics as your terminal sessions.

mode.label: Mode
mode.conversationHint: Multi-turn — keep typing follow-ups while the agent works.
mode.headlessHint: Single prompt, single response. Stdin closes after spawn.

fields.cwdHint: Absolute path. Defaults to the dashboard's own cwd.
fields.cwdPlaceholder: Type to search or paste an absolute path…
fields.cwdNoMatches: No matches. Type an absolute path to use it directly.
fields.permissionDefault: default (interactive prompts — sessions may hang headlessly)
fields.effortHint: Drives how much the model reasons before answering. Higher = more thinking tokens, slower, deeper. Empty inherits the model's default.

binary.missing: The `claude` CLI isn't on your PATH. Install Claude Code or set PATH so the dashboard can spawn it.

concurrency.atCap: Concurrency cap reached ({{max}}). Stop one of the running runs to start a new one.

hint.permissionWarning: bypassPermissions allows ALL tool invocations without confirming. Only use for trusted prompts.
hint.headlessExplain: Headless mode is best for scripted tasks where you know exactly what you want. The session can't ask follow-up questions and will hang on permission prompts unless you stay in acceptEdits.

empty.title: Nothing yet
empty.body: Type a prompt and click Run. Output streams here as the model thinks, calls tools, and answers.

runs.scopeNote: Only shows runs you started from this dashboard.

resume.label: Source session
resume.freshHint: Start a fresh Claude Code session.
resume.resumeHint: Pick a session from your history and continue the conversation. Cwd is locked to the original.
resume.search: Search by ID, cwd, or status…

limitations.title: Running Claude in your browser
limitations.subtitle: same binary, different surface
limitations.peek: Most TUI features carry over. A handful of interactive ones don't.
limitations.intro: Under the hood we spawn the same `claude` binary your terminal uses — but stream-json (the only programmatic interface Claude exposes) is a one-way fire-hose, not the interactive TUI. Anything that depends on a running terminal session — in-place permission prompts, the keybinding overlay, mid-session config changes — has to be set up at spawn time instead of mid-conversation.
limitations.tldr: If a feature isn't here, pop the same session open in your terminal with `claude --resume <session-id>` — both sides share the same JSONL transcript on disk, so you never lose context by switching surfaces.

autocomplete.groupBuiltin: Built-in (CLI only — won't run from here)
